text:
REGIME — Narrow risk-on, large-cap dispersion high, defensive rotation visible.

INDICES — VIX 25.8, elevated and rising. GLD led +0.97%, IJR +0.33%, IWM +0.22%, IJH +0.13%, SPY +0.04%, QQQ +0.02%, DIA -0.19%, RSP -0.22%. Small caps outperformed large caps. VIXY surged +3.52%, the sharpest cross-asset move, signaling persistent hedging demand despite modest equity gains.

SECTORS — XLE +0.80%, XLK +0.48%, XLU +0.22%, XLV +0.20%, XLC +0.05%, XLF +0.00%, XLRE -0.10%, XLI -0.20%, XLB -0.28%, XLY -1.16%, XLP -1.69%. Top industries: WGMI +2.37%, XES +2.08%. Bottom industries: XHB -2.23%, MSOS -8.56%.

THE 7s — Buildout +1.55%, Semi +1.43%, Old Metals +1.14%, Fab +0.79%, Energy +0.71%, Health +0.53%, Power +0.36%, Cloud +0.33%, Bank +0.23%, Industrial +0.07%, New Staples -0.06%, Insurance -0.10%, Freight -0.18%, Software -0.25%, Medtech -0.25%, Toll Road -0.35%, Defense -1.02%, New Metals -1.03%, Old Staples -1.90%, Housing -1.91%. Strongest ticker in Buildout 7 not specified in data; weakest in Housing 7 not individually named — Housing 7 led losses at -1.91%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.2, Neutral. Top movers: UNH +9.37% (Health 7, 2.5x vol spike), MSOS -8.56% (industries), RSPS -1.58% (3.0x vol spike). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +7.52%; worst: XLV -4.59%. Best basket 20d: Energy +9.02%; worst: Software -9.85%. UNH's +9.37% surge on earnings stability drove XLV's outperformance relative to its -4.59% 20d drag.

WATCH — UNH +9.37%, 2.5x vol spike; gap-up on earnings catalyst warrants fade-vs-continuation read tomorrow. RSPS -1.58%, 3.0x vol spike; equal-weight staples diverging sharply from XLP, watch for institutional reallocation signal. XHB -2.23%; housing momentum deteriorating at -6.53% on 20d with Housing 7 at -1.91% today, rate sensitivity increasing.
